seoTitle: Torquay vs Torrens Creek — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Torquay and Torrens Creek. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Torquay vs Torrens Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Torquay and Torrens Creek.
heading: Torquay vs Torrens Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Torquay, Queensland and Torrens Creek, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Torrens Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (23/100) compared to Torquay (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Torquay or Torrens Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Torquay has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Torrens Creek scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Torquay scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Torrens Creek scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Torquay or Torrens Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Torrens Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.32% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
